title: Moving house
explanation:
Moving house

Listen to two friends talking about a recent move.

Transcript

Sam: How is the new flat?
Nina: Much better than the old one. It is closer to work, but it is smaller.
Sam: Was it difficult to move?
Nina: Yes, especially carrying the boxes up three flights of stairs. But the neighbours were friendly and helped us.

questions.text: Nina says the new flat is [*closer to work|bigger|more expensive].
questions.feedback: She says it is closer to work.

questions.text: Moving was difficult because of the [*stairs|weather|traffic].
questions.feedback: They had to carry boxes upstairs.

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: Nina thinks the new flat is ____ than the old one. | *better | worse | older
questions.feedback: She says it is much better.

questions.text: They carried the boxes up three flights of ____.  | *stairs | lifts | roads
questions.feedback: The boxes went up the stairs.

questions.text: The neighbours ____ them with the move. | *helped | ignored | phoned
The move was difficult, ____. | *especially carrying boxes | especially finding work | especially buying furniture
The conversation is mainly about [*a recent move|a holiday|a job interview].
Nina compares the new flat with the [*old one|next one|office].
questions.feedback: She compares it with the old flat.

questions.text: Sam asks whether it was [*difficult|cheap|safe] to move.

questions.feedback: Despite the difficulties, Nina likes the new flat.
